content.problemStatement:
You are given two sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ and B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.

You need to answer QQQ queries. In each query you are given an integer XXX and you need find the sum of all elements BiB_iBi​ of the sequence BBB for which it holds that Ai≤XA_i \leq XAi​≤X.


Input Format


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.




The first line of each test case contains two-space separated integers NNN and QQQ.




The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.




The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




The fourth line contains QQQ space-separated integers X1,X2,…,XQX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_QX1​,X2​,…,XQ​.





Output Format

For each test case, print a single line containing QQQ space-separated integers. The jjj-th integer in the line should be the sum of all elements BiB_iBi​ of the sequence BBB for which it holds that Ai≤XjA_i \leq X_jAi​≤Xj​.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
Sum of Q over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and Q.
- The second line contains N integers A1 … AN.
- The third line contains N integers B1 … BN.
- The fourth line contains Q integers X1 … XQ.
All numbers are separated by single spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with Q space‑separated integers. The j‑th integer is the sum of all Bi such that Ai ≤ Xj.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each query X we sum B_i where A_i ≤ X. The sums are: X=1 → 9, X=2 → 9+9=18, X=3 → 9+9=18, X=9 → 9+9+8+9+1=36, X=5 → 9+9+1=19.
X=5 includes no A, sum=0. X=25 includes A=10,20 → B=1+2=3. X=100 includes all → B=1+2+3+4=10.

solution.approach: For each test case pair each Ai with its Bi, sort the pairs by Ai in non‑decreasing order and build a prefix sum array pref where pref[i] = sum of B of the first i elements in the sorted order. For a query X we need the sum of B for all Ai ≤ X, which is exactly pref[pos] where pos = upper_bound(sorted_A, X) (the number of elements ≤ X). The answer for each query is obtained in O(log N) time using binary search. Overall complexity per test case is O(N log N + Q log N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N log N + Q log N) per test case
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) per test case for the sorted arrays and prefix sums

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
import sys, bisect

def solve():
    data = list(map(int, sys.stdin.read().strip().split()))
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = next(it)
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = next(it); Q = next(it)
        A = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        B = [next(it) for _ in range(N)]
        pairs = sorted(zip(A, B))
        pref = [0]
        for _, b in pairs:
            pref.append(pref[-1] + b)
        As = [a for a, _ in pairs]
        answers = []
        for _ in range(Q):
            X = next(it)
            idx = bisect.bisect_right(As, X)
            answers.append(str(pref[idx]))
        out_lines.append(' '.join(answers))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
